title: Failure Classification, Retry Budgets, Backoff, and Load Shedding · Type-System Trace: Retry Budget Deadline
summary: Every exception is requeued immediately, permanent schema failures block healthy work, a database outage causes a retry storm, and attempt counts exist only in process memory. Planned artifact: Add and verify the retry budget deadline increment in failure taxonomy and bounded retry policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget.
Classify malformed, unauthorized, missing dependency, rate limit, timeout, conflict, transient, permanent, and ambiguous effect failures by evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Retry Budget Deadline · Theory Retry Budget Deadline
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6687 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify permanent and ambiguous outcomes before applying both attempt and age budgets; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget.
